metadata.name: Mcp
metadata.description: Tools > MCP server overview. The typed API surface the agents use to inspect every JsonUI project. 29 tools in four groups: A (lookup), B (validation), C (generation), D (build + runtime). ~12-min read. Companion to /tools/agents — agents are the clients, MCP is the protocol.

uiVariables.name: tools
uiVariables.description: 29 MCP tools. Each entry names the tool, declares its group (A/B/C/D), and a one-line role.

uiVariables.name: nextReadLinks
uiVariables.description: Two closing cards: Agents (client of MCP) + CLI (caller behind jui build).

eventHandlers.name: onAppear
eventHandlers.description: Seed tools + nextReadLinks from the static v1 catalog.

dataFlow.diagram:
flowchart TD
    VIEW[McpView] --> VM[McpViewModel]
    VM -- tools --> VIEW
    VM -- nextReadLinks --> VIEW
    VIEW -- onNavigate(url) --> VM

methods.name: onAppear
methods.description: Seed tools + nextReadLinks. tools carries 29 MCP tool entries grouped A/B/C/D by function (Discovery / Authoring / Generation / Gate). Each row's nameKey / groupKey / roleKey is pre-resolved via StringManager with the tools_mcp_ prefix. nextReadLinks points to /tools/agents and /tools/cli.

methods.name: onNavigate
methods.description: router.push(url). Hit by NextReadLink card taps.

vars.name: tools
vars.description: 29 MCP tool rows, grouped by role (A/B/C/D).

transitions.condition: url is a spec-mapped tools URL or /

relatedFiles.description: One-line MCP tool row with name / group / role.

notes: Second live entry under the Tools tab. Flipping TOOLS_ENTRIES row 2 (mcp) from 'upcoming' to 'live' in HomeViewModel finishes the task.

29 tools total. Group A = 7 (lookup), Group B = 6 (validation), Group C = 7 (generation), Group D = 9 (build + runtime). Per-tool prose lives on /reference/mcp-tools (upcoming); this page is the overview.
The cell uses a generic mcp_tool_row layout, not the agent_row layout — even though the visual is similar, the group pill differs in semantics (A/B/C/D letter code rather than a named role).
